item.title: Few titles on language
item.description: A few new titles on the historic 1952 Language Movement and related topics were published at the ongoing Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026....

item.title: Wages, safety of construction workers demanded before Eid
item.description: Leaders of Bangladesh Building Construction Workers Federation on Monday at a rally in Dhaka demanded that construction workers be paid their wages and festival allowances before Eid-ul-Fitr....Geographic Reference

item.title: Prof FM Siddiqui appointed Bangladesh Medical University VC
item.description: Dr FM Siddiqui has been appointed vice-chancellor of Bangladesh Medical University....

item.title: 37,567 selected for family card
item.description: The government has selected 37,567 female-head families to be given with the newly introduced family card....

item.title: 33 more ME flights cancelled
item.description: At least 33 more Middle Eastern flights were cancelled to and from the Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka on Monday, raising the total number of flights cancelled to 335 so far since February 28....

item.title: Bangladeshis’ death toll from ME war reaches 4, Dubai migrant’s body arrives home
item.description: The body of a Bangladeshi migrant killed in a missile strike in Dubai of the United Arab Emirates amid the ongoing Middle East conflict arrived in Bangladesh on Monday when the government confirmed one more death in Saudi Arabia raising the......

item.title: 12 held, arms recovered in massive raid at Ctg Jungle Salimpur
item.description: A huge team of the joint forces on Monday conducted a massive special operation at Jungle Salimpur under Sitakunda upazila of Chattogram, detaining 12 people and recovering arms, explosives, and surveillance equipment....

item.title: Another killed during arbitration
item.description: A man was beaten to death and four others were injured in a clash during a village arbitration at Shibganj upazila in Chapainawabganj district on Sunday night....

item.title: Global arms exports soar on European demand: study
item.description: Global weapons flows grew by almost 10 per cent in the past five years, with Europe more than tripling imports, a report showed on Monday....

item.title: Coaching business rampant despite ban
item.description: Coaching business is going unabated across the country despite different restrictions slapped by the government against the practice over the years. ...

item.title: Washback effect in English classrooms
item.description: While public examinations form a major component of the educational structure in Bangladesh, the Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) is seen as a watershed juncture in the life of a student. It irrevocably affects their academic path, university admissions, and subsequent career choices. Given these high stakes, the examination regime is inexorably woven into the......

item.title: Missile maths: who runs out first?
item.description: THE United States began Israel’s ‘war of choice’ on February 28. While framed as an American operation, it is, in essence, Israel’s war that the United States is fighting and funding. The justification for this pre-emptive strike, as articulated by the US secretary of state, shows a striking paradox: ‘Iran did not pose an imminent threat to the USA. Israel was planning to attack......

item.title: Strategic misreading of Iran
item.description: BENEATH the visible theatre of warfare, a more consequential dynamic appears to be unfolding. It is one that suggests the United States may have misjudged not merely Iran’s capabilities, but the nature of the war itself......

item.title: Redefining development leadership
item.description: ON MANY policy tables in Dhaka, the question is no longer how to receive development assistance, but how to shape development direction. For decades, countries like Bangladesh were seen primarily as recipients of ideas, models and funding designed elsewhere. Today, the narrative is shifting. Development leadership increasingly means moving from absorbing......City & Local Guides

item.title: Govt needs to act to curb rising thalassaemia
item.description: THE gradual spread of thalassaemia is worrying. Bureau of Statistics data show that 11.4 per cent of the people were carriers of the disease in 2024, which is considered a significantly high rate as the global percentage is 1.5. The percentage was only 4 in 2000. This now comes down to about 20 million thalassaemia carriers. The Bangladesh Thalassaemia Foundation estimates......

item.title: Collective suffering of Bawm community must end
item.description: THE prolonged arbitrary detention of members of the Bawm community is a grave violation of human rights. Around 200 Bawm civilians, including 26 women and four children, were subjected to arbitrary detention in April 2024 after the robbery of two banks at Ruma and Thanchi in Bandarban, allegedly carried out by the Kuki-Chin National Front, an armed group in......

item.title: Tk 1,064cr highway project set to improve connectivity in Ctg hill districts
item.description: The government has undertaken a Tk 1,064.26 crore project to widen and upgrade Rangamati’s Manikchhari–Mahalchhari–Khagrachari Regional Highway in a bid to improve road safety, strengthen connectivity and boost economic activities in the......

item.title: Farmers hope for bumper harvest of boro in Rangpur
item.description: Tender boro rice plants are now growing magnificently in the favourable climate of the Rangpur agricultural region, raising hopes among farmers for a bumper crop yield this season....

item.title: 15 hurt with bullet in supremacy clash in Munshiganj
item.description: The police arrested three people and recovered firearms and ammunition on Sunday night, after at least 15 people, including several women, were shot at in a clash between rival groups over dominance at Srinagar in Munshiganj that night....

item.title: 7 Jhenaidah SAD leaders held over vandalism, arson
item.description: THE police arrested seven leaders of the Students Against Discrimination, which led the July Uprising in 2024, from different areas in Jhenaidah on allegations of bus arson and vandalism at fuel pumps....

item.title: DU student beaten over suspected BCL link
item.description: A student of the University of Dhaka was beaten allegedly over his involvement with the banned Bangladesh Chhatra League on the Dhaka University campus early Monday....Geographic Reference

item.title: BNP PP meeting tomorrow
item.description: A parliamentary party meeting of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party will be held on Wednesday at the Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban ahead of the first JS session on March 12....

item.title: Acting Krishak League president Jahangir arrested
item.description: The police have arrested Bangladesh Krishak League’s acting president Sheikh Md Jahangir Alam from the capital’s Adabar area in connection with a case of attacking and assaulting people during the student-led July mass uprising in 2024 at Shyamoli....

item.title: 10 kidnappers held in Sylhet, 1 victim rescued
item.description: Ten youths, including 9 teenagers, were arrested on Monday at a residence in Sylhet on charges of kidnapping and extorting ransom from the victim....

item.title: ASI attacked during patrol, pistol snatched
item.description: Three unidentified people attacked an on-duty assistant sub-inspector and snatched his service pistol in Mandalpara area in Narayanganj city on Monday morning....

item.title: DC confce likely to begin May 3
item.description: The 2026 annual conference of deputy commissioners is set to begin on May 3 in Dhaka and is planned to continue for four days until May 6....

item.title: Youth killed at Jatrabari over drug dispute
item.description: The police on Monday recovered the body of a young man who was killed after his leg veins were cut in the capital’s Jatrabari area, reportedly following a dispute over drug trading....

item.title: Ferry services on Paturia-Daulatdia route resume after 3 hours
item.description: Ferry services on the Paturia-Daulatdia route resumed on Monday morning after remaining suspended for about three hours due to dense fog....

item.title: AIUB holds webinar on computational social science
item.description: The department of journalism and mass communication at the American International University–Bangladesh has recently organised a webinar titled Messy Data, Big Questions: Social Science in the Computational Age....

item.title: Pro-BNP teachers demand resignation of JnU VC
item.description: A teachers’ organisation aligned with the Bangladesh Nationalist Party has called for the resignation of Jagannath University’s vice-chancellor, professor Rezaul Karim, accusing him of failing to address key institutional issues and contributing to instability on campus....

item.title: Onion farmers stare at losses despite bumper yield
item.description: Despite a record yield, onion farmers in the country are staring at financial losses this season as market prices have fallen below production costs at the beginning of the harvesting period....

item.title: Coast Guard arrests 2 ‘smugglers’ with illegal cigarettes in Ctg
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested two smugglers with 30,270 packets of foreign cigarettes worth Tk 1.21 crore and seized a cargo boat used for smuggling at Patenga in Chattogram on Sunday....

item.title: Universal hospital provides women with free treatment
item.description: Universal Medical College Hospital observed ‘Free Women’s Health Day’ on Sunday at the hospital at Mohakhali in the capital on the occasion of ‘International Women’s Day’....

item.title: Meeting on uninterrupted supply chain held at Ctg Port
item.description: A coordination meeting was held at the conference room of the Chittagong Port Authority on Sunday under the chairmanship of Rear Admiral SM Moniruzzaman, chairman, on the uninterrupted supply chain and bunkering of fuel oil....
